rationale: The citing paper uses Yang, Toubia, and De Jong (2015) in a functional way beyond background mention. It explicitly positions its contribution relative to that prior joint search-choice modeling approach, stating that it does not build a joint model like Yang et al. It also reuses the Yang et al. laptop eye-tracking conjoint study as a conceptual replication dataset and points readers there for design details, with replication results integrated into core tables and discussion. This is a meaningful empirical and positioning role, but not a direct extension or methodological application of the cited model itself.
relation_description: The citing paper complements and contrasts with Yang et al. (2015) by focusing on attentional-process characterization rather than joint search-choice modeling, while using Yang et al.'s laptop study data as one of its replication contexts. Thus, the cited paper serves as a substantive prior anchor and empirical source for replication evidence.
